briefing.one_line_description: Provider of identity and access management solutions for workforce, customers, and AI agents

recent_signals.description: Released Q1 2025 Platform updates focusing on AI agent authorization and phishing protection
Raymond James upgraded stock to Outperform, shares rose 6% on improved profitability outlook
Barclays upgraded to Overweight with $85 price target, average analyst target $100.58
Invested in Knox Systems, Replit, and Sapiom as part of strategic investment activity
Launched AI agent security solutions across full lifecycle with neutral extensible platform

data_gaps: Current C-suite executives (CEO, CFO, CTO) not identified
Exact IPO date and valuation details
Most recent funding round details (appears to be public since before 2025)
Specific hiring signals or headcount growth trends

dossier.disqualifier_reason: Core business model is not software — Okta is a cybersecurity/identity management company, not a B2B SaaS company selling sales engagement, pipeline intelligence, or revenue operations tooling. While they sell software, their product category (identity and access management) does not align with the ICP's focus on sales/RevOps platforms.
dossier.tier_reasoning: Okta is disqualified because their core business is identity and access management (cybersecurity), not sales/RevOps tooling. They are not a prospect for a sales engagement or pipeline intelligence platform — they would be selling to security/IT buyers, not sales/RevOps leaders. This is a category mismatch, not a competitor issue.
icp_matches: Clearly B2B SaaS with subscription revenue model
Public company with established market presence
Has visible sales organization (VP Sales identified)
icp_gaps: Wrong product category — sells identity/access management, not sales/RevOps tools
5,908 employees — outside the 50-2,000 sweet spot, enterprise scale
Sells to security/IT buyers, not sales/RevOps leaders
No visible sales hiring signals or GTM expansion in the briefing
